title: [HW] `hw-flatten-modules` can't find an module that exists

features.name: hw.module private with comb logic body
features.description: Code should include a hw.module marked private containing combinational logic operations (comb.xor, comb.and) with i1 ports, representing a private sub-module that may be referenced by instantiation

features.name: hw.instance referencing private module
features.description: Code should include a hw.instance operation that instantiates a privately-scoped hw.module by symbol reference, passing i1 operands and binding named result ports
features.snippet: %s_1, %c_2 = hw.instance "h2" @HalfAdder(a: %p2_1: i1, b: %p3_0: i1) -> (s: i1, co: i1)

features.name: verif.formal and hw.module private coexistence in same compilation unit
features.description: Code should include both verif.formal operations and hw.module private definitions in the same MLIR file, such that running the hw-flatten-modules pass after lower-contracts triggers a module-not-found error for the private module
